LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RYAN MOONEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND TO VACATE ORDER FOR APPEARANCE AND EXAM IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION STATES THAT THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS BASED ON CCP SECTIONS 415.20, 415.90, 473(B), AND 587. HOWEVER, IN HIS REPLY BRIEF, DEFENDANT CONCEDES THAT HE ERRONEOUSLY CITED CCP 473(B) AS THE BASIS FOR RELIEF, AND "INSTEAD IS CONTENDING THAT THIS COURT DOES NOT HAVE JURISDICTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT TO BE ENTERED AS HE WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED AND DID NOT HAVE ACTUAL NOTICE." (DEFENDANT'S REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND TO VACATE ORDER FOR APPEARANCE AND EXAMINATION, 6:18-19.) DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT CITE TO ANY APPLICABLE STATUTES TO GRANT RELIEF ON THIS BASIS. THE COURT CANNOT GRANT DIFFERENT RELIEF, OR RELIEF ON DIFFERENT GROUNDS, THAN STATED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. (SEE LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 1119, 1124.) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO VACATE THE ORDER FOR APPEARANCE AND EXAMINATION IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DOUGLAS PROBENCHER, ESQ., 707.284.2380, FOR PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION.
KEVIN BARGER, ESQ., 510.285.0747M FOR DEFENDANT RYAN MOONEY.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT RYAN MOONEY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND TO VACATE ORDER FOR APPEARANCE AND EXAM is denied without prejudice. Defendant's notice of motion states that the motion to set aside default is based on CCP Sections 415.20, 415.90, 473(b), and 587. However, in his Reply brief, Defendant concedes that he erroneously cited CCP 473(b) as the basis for relief, and "instead is contending that this court does not have jurisdiction for default judgment to be entered as he was not properly served and did not have actual notice." (Defendant's Reply Brief in Support of Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment and to Vacate Order for Appearance and Examination, 6:18-19.) Defendant's Notice of Motion does not cite to any applicable statutes to grant relief on this basis. The court cannot grant different relief, or relief on different grounds, than stated in the notice of motion. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1124.) Defendant's request to vacate the Order for Appearance and Examination is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
